     `pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/glossary.ts > docs/cli-command-glossary.md` to refresh -->

# CLI Command Glossary

Per-command reference for every `sittir` sub-command. This document is
generated from the live commander program tree; run the command above to
refresh after adding or modifying commands.

---

## `gen`

Generate typed factories, templates, and native bindings from a grammar

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-o, --output <dir>` ‚Äî Output directory
- `-n, --nodes <list>` ‚Äî Comma-separated node kinds to generate
- `-a, --all` ‚Äî Generate TS + native render-module artifacts (full chain)
- `--tests-dir <dir>` ‚Äî Output directory for test files
- `--transpile` ‚Äî Transpile overrides.ts ‚Üí .sittir/grammar.js
- `--compile-parser` ‚Äî Compile override grammar to .sittir/parser.wasm
- `--ts-generate` ‚Äî Run 'tree-sitter generate' in .sittir/
- `--skip-ts-chain` ‚Äî Skip the auto transpile + tree-sitter generate chain
- `--roundtrip` ‚Äî Run validator probes after generation
- `--no-build-native` ‚Äî Skip the post-regen N-API rebuild
- `--no-workspace-check` ‚Äî Skip the post-build cargo check --workspace (multi-grammar drivers run it once, on the last grammar)
- `--no-emit-diff` ‚Äî Suppress the post-regen emit diff
- `--allow-diagnostic <code>` ‚Äî Allow a blocking grammar diagnostic (repeatable) (default: ``)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts gen [options]
```

---

## `validate`

Validation utilities for sittir grammar packages

### `validate counts`

Per-grammar raw pass/total counts for all four validators

**Arguments**

- `grammars‚Ä¶` (optional) ‚Äî Grammars to validate; defaults to all

**Options**

- `--isolate` ‚Äî Run each grammar in a child process; attributes SIGSEGV to the last attempted kind (default: `false`)
- `--_isolate-worker` ‚Äî Internal: run as an isolation worker for a single grammar (default: `false`)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts validate counts [grammars] [options]
```

### `validate probe-factory`

Factory-render-parse error bucketing (top-8 buckets)

**Arguments**

- `grammars‚Ä¶` (optional) ‚Äî Grammars to validate; defaults to all

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts validate probe-factory [grammars] [options]
```

### `validate history`

Print last N validation history entries

**Arguments**

- `n` (optional) ‚Äî Number of entries to show

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts validate history [n] [options]
```

### `validate trace-rt`

Replay the first failing read-render-parse case as a rich trace

**Arguments**

- `grammar` (required) ‚Äî Grammar to trace (rust, typescript, python)

**Options**

- `-r, --recursive` ‚Äî Use recursive deep-read instead of shallow (default: `false`)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts validate trace-rt <grammar> [options]
```

## `tool`

Developer diagnostics

### `tool bench`

Render benchmark comparing native (Askama) vs JS (Nunjucks)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool bench [options]
```

### `tool bench-codemod`

Benchmark codemod on a corpus directory

**Arguments**

- `corpus-dir` (required) ‚Äî Corpus directory to benchmark

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool bench-codemod <corpus-dir> [options]
```

### `tool check-baseline`

Check or collect parity baselines

**Options**

- `--collect` ‚Äî Run collect-baseline (collect fresh baseline to stdout)
- `--backend <backend>` ‚Äî Backend for collect mode: native | js (default: js) (default: `js`)
- `--metrics` ‚Äî Emit metrics file when SITTIR_METRICS=1 is set (collect mode only)
- `--base <path>` ‚Äî Base baseline JSON path (check mode)
- `--head <path>` ‚Äî Head baseline JSON path (check mode)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool check-baseline [options]
```

### `tool check-jinja`

Validate .jinja template files (headers, no YAML regressions)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool check-jinja [options]
```

### `tool check-perf`

Check native FFI performance against committed baseline

**Options**

- `--baseline <path>` ‚Äî Path to committed PerfBaseline JSON
- `--metrics <path>` ‚Äî Path to freshly produced MetricsFile JSON (default: ./metrics-native.json)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool check-perf [options]
```

### `tool classify`

Inspect kind classification through the compiler phases

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--kind <name>` ‚Äî Show only this kind (repeatable) (default: ``)
- `--modeltype <type>` ‚Äî Filter output to this modelType
- `--all` ‚Äî Show all assembled kinds

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool classify [options]
```

### `tool compare-overrides`

Compare override key sets between backup and current overrides.suggested.ts

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar(s) to compare: rust|python|typescript|all (default: `all`)
- `--backup-dir <dir>` ‚Äî Directory containing <grammar>-overrides.ts backup files
- `--backup-rust <file>` ‚Äî Path to Rust overrides backup file
- `--backup-python <file>` ‚Äî Path to Python overrides backup file
- `--backup-typescript <file>` ‚Äî Path to TypeScript overrides backup file
- `--suggested-dir <dir>` ‚Äî Packages root for overrides.suggested.ts lookup

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool compare-overrides [options]
```

### `tool diff-failures`

Dump per-kind validator failures for a grammar

**Arguments**

- `which` (optional) ‚Äî Which validators: all | from | rt | cov | factory

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool diff-failures [which] [options]
```

### `tool dump-ast-mismatches`

Diagnostic for read-render-parse AST gaps

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--all-grammars` ‚Äî Run all three grammars
- `-m, --mode <mode>` ‚Äî Mode: deep | shallow | diff (default: `deep`)
- `-f, --filter <substr>` ‚Äî Restrict to entries whose name contains substr
- `-c, --cluster` ‚Äî Group mismatches by message pattern
- `--format <fmt>` ‚Äî Output format: list | json (default: `list`)
- `-v, --verbose` ‚Äî Print INPUT/RENDERED bodies for every mismatch

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool dump-ast-mismatches [options]
```

### `tool exercise`

Exercise factory round-trips with built-in or corpus cases

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-k, --kinds <kind,...>` ‚Äî Comma-separated kind list to exercise

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool exercise [options]
```

### `tool field-provenance`

Field source tracking (override/enriched/grammar)

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-k, --kind <K>` ‚Äî Filter to a single rule kind
- `--redundant` ‚Äî Only print redundant-nested FIELD rows
- `--source <src>` ‚Äî Filter by source tag: override|enriched|grammar|inferred

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool field-provenance [options]
```

### `tool grammar-diagnostics`

Run pre-codegen grammar diagnostics

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool grammar-diagnostics [options]
```

### `tool inspect-refs`

Inspect symbol references or derivation suggestions

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--mode <mode>` ‚Äî Mode: refs | suggestions (default: `refs`)
- `--symbol <name>` ‚Äî Target symbol name (refs mode) (default: `_type_identifier`)
- `--base` ‚Äî Use base grammar.js instead of overrides
- `--limit <n>` ‚Äî Max entries per section (suggestions mode) (default: `10`)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool inspect-refs [options]
```

### `tool inspect-type`

Inspect Loose/Config type shapes via the TS compiler API

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--entry <kindNs>` ‚Äî Single namespace interface to inspect
- `--namespaces <ns,...>` ‚Äî Comma-separated list of namespaces (overrides --entry)
- `--slots` ‚Äî Print each slot type on the resolved Loose shape
- `--config` ‚Äî Print Config shape for comparison

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool inspect-type [options]
```

### `tool list-kinds`

List groups, unaliased, and phantom kinds

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--groups` ‚Äî List all group-modelled kinds
- `--unaliased` ‚Äî List groups with no visible non-group twin
- `--phantom` ‚Äî List phantom kinds (nodeMap without a parser symbol)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool list-kinds [options]
```

### `tool phantom-kinds`

Enumerate codegen kinds with no parser symbol across grammars

**Arguments**

- `grammars‚Ä¶` (optional) ‚Äî Grammars to check (default: all three)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool phantom-kinds [grammars] [options]
```

### `tool probe-kind`

Structured diagnostics for parse ‚Üí readNode ‚Üí render cycle

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-s, --source <text>` ‚Äî Source text to probe
- `--stdin` ‚Äî Read source from stdin
- `-k, --kind <kind>` ‚Äî Find first node of this kind and probe it
- `--range <start,end>` ‚Äî Probe node at byte range start,end
- `--no-render` ‚Äî Skip the render pass
- `--no-wrap` ‚Äî Use core readNode directly (skip grammar readTreeNode)
- `--reparse` ‚Äî Render ‚Üí re-parse ‚Üí include reparsed CST
- `--pretty` ‚Äî Pretty-print JSON output (2-space indent)
- `--baseline <dir>` ‚Äî Compare against a staged baseline package dir
- `--baseline-parser` ‚Äî Use baseline parser.wasm instead of current
- `--engine <engine>` ‚Äî Render engine: js | native | both (default: native)
- `--trace` ‚Äî Emit full multi-lane trace (js + native, shallow + deep)
- `--log-parse` ‚Äî Log tree-sitter parse events to stderr
- `--full` ‚Äî Emit complete multi-lane trace (like --trace)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool probe-kind [options]
```

### `tool probe-parity`

Probe read-render-parse coverage for a target kind

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-t, --target <kind>` ‚Äî Target kind to check coverage for (default: `visibility_modifier`)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool probe-parity [options]
```

### `tool probe-stages`

Dump a rule shape at every compiler phase

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-k, --kind <kind>` ‚Äî Rule kind to probe
- `--no-overrides` ‚Äî Skip overrides.ts, use base grammar.js directly
- `--compact` ‚Äî Compact JSON output (no indent)
- `--skip-emit` ‚Äî Skip the emit phases (types + template)
- `--brief` ‚Äî Print only grammar, kind, and simplify fields

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool probe-stages [options]
```

### `tool probe-validate`

Probe a corpus entry through read ‚Üí wrap ‚Üí render pipeline

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-e, --entry <name>` ‚Äî Corpus entry name (exact match)
- `--entry-pattern <regex>` ‚Äî Corpus entry name (regex match, first hit)
- `--first-failing` ‚Äî Probe the first RT-failing entry for the grammar
- `-s, --source <text>` ‚Äî Source text to probe (same as probe-kind)
- `--stdin` ‚Äî Read source from stdin
- `--engine <engine>` ‚Äî Render engine: js | native | both (default: native)
- `--trace` ‚Äî Emit full multi-lane trace
- `--pretty` ‚Äî Pretty-print JSON output
- `--no-render` ‚Äî Skip the render pass
- `--no-wrap` ‚Äî Use core readNode directly (skip grammar readTreeNode)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool probe-validate [options]
```

### `tool profile`

Aggregate validator failures across grammars

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--top <n>` ‚Äî Number of top patterns (default: `15`)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool profile [options]
```

### `tool profile-factory`

Profile factory-render-parse failures

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--recursive` ‚Äî Use recursive deep-read instead of shallow
- `--ast` ‚Äî Include AST mismatch breakdown

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool profile-factory [options]
```

### `tool propose-14`

Principle #14 signature-conformance ratchet (classify pipeline fns; fail on baseline increase)

**Options**

- `--update` ‚Äî Rewrite the committed baseline to the current counts
- `--table` ‚Äî Print the per-function classification table (drives R1-R4 + R8)
- `--json` ‚Äî Machine-readable JSON output
- `--baseline <path>` ‚Äî Baseline path (default: packages/codegen/.principle14-baseline.json)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool propose-14 [options]
```

### `tool variant-derivation-probe`

Assert the live structural variantChildKinds derivation equals committed node-model.json5 (cross-commit drift detector)

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `--all-grammars` ‚Äî Run every grammar (rust, typescript, python)

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool variant-derivation-probe [options]
```

### `tool walk`

Walk a parsed tree and print kind counts

**Options**

- `-g, --grammar <name>` ‚Äî Grammar to operate on ‚Äî choices: `rust` | `typescript` | `python`
- `-s, --source <text>` ‚Äî Source text to parse
- `--render` ‚Äî Render each visited node

**Example**

```sh
pnpm exec tsx packages/cli/src/cli.ts tool walk [options]
```
